workItems.title: Basic Pay Setup and Computation
Create and maintain employee basic pay setup
workItems.persona: As a P&A Admin or Client Admin
workItems.need: to create, view, edit, inactivate, and audit basic pay setup per employee or employee group
workItems.benefit: payroll transactions use the correct basic pay rate for each employee.
workItems.description: As a P&A Admin or Client Admin to create, view, edit, inactivate, and audit basic pay setup per employee or employee group payroll transactions use the correct basic pay rate for each employee.
workItems.sourceTrace: Primary: PDF earning setup/pages 5-9; Secondary: Annex A payroll setup and BRD basic pay discussion.
acceptanceCriteria: Given I have setup access, when I create a basic pay record, then the system requires employee/employee group, payment mode, basic pay amount, effective date, work days/factor days, status, and remarks where applicable.

Given a duplicate active basic pay exists for the same employee and overlapping effective period, when I save, then the system blocks the duplicate or requires end-dating/inactivation of the old setup.
Given the basic pay record is used in a posted payroll transaction, when a user attempts hard delete, then the system prevents hard delete and allows only inactive/end-dated status with audit trail.

Given I update the basic pay amount or effective date, when I save, then the system writes old value/new value audit history and flags related open payroll transactions for recalculation if applicable.
Given I lack edit access, when I open the setup screen, then I can only view/download according to my role rights.
qaFocus: Test valid monthly, daily, hourly, semi-monthly, and weekly setup where reference values are available.
Test overlapping effective dates and confirm system blocks or requires end-dating.
Test inactive records are not pulled into future payroll but remain visible in history/reporting.
Test audit log contains user, timestamp, action, old value, new value, and remarks.

workItems.title: Automatically derive annual, daily, hourly, and per-minute rates
workItems.need: the system to compute derived rates from basic pay setup
workItems.benefit: earnings, deductions, statutory bases, and reports use one consistent rate source.
workItems.description: As a Payroll Admin the system to compute derived rates from basic pay setup earnings, deductions, statutory bases, and reports use one consistent rate source.
workItems.sourceTrace: Primary: PDF computation basis/pages 5-8; Secondary: Annex A and BRD pay mode/monthly-to-daily/hourly discussion.
acceptanceCriteria: Given payment mode is Monthly and monthly basic pay is entered, when I save, then Annual Rate = Monthly Basic Pay x 12.
Given Annual Rate and Work Days per Year exist, when I save, then Daily Rate = Annual Rate / Work Days per Year rounded according to payroll decimal rules.
Given Daily Rate and Required Work Hours per Day exist, when I save, then Hourly Rate = Daily Rate / Required Work Hours per Day and Per-Minute Rate = Hourly Rate / 60.
Given required work days or work hours are missing, when the formula requires them, then the system blocks save or marks rate derivation incomplete with clear error messaging.
Given payment mode is not monthly, when the rate is saved, then the system uses the configured computational basis for that payment mode and stores the derived rate values used by payroll.
qaFocus: Use sample: Monthly Basic Pay 20,000; Work Days 261; Required Hours 8; expected Daily Rate roughly 919.54 and Hourly Rate roughly 114.94.
Test leap/yearly factor changes where Work Days per Year changes.
Test a rate update after an open payroll transaction and ensure transaction can be recalculated or flagged.

workItems.title: Pull basic pay into payroll transaction with Zero Basic Pay option
workItems.need: payroll transactions to pull configured basic pay by default and allow controlled zero-basic-pay processing
workItems.benefit: special payroll/final pay scenarios can be processed without manual cleanup.
workItems.description: As a Payroll Processor payroll transactions to pull configured basic pay by default and allow controlled zero-basic-pay processing special payroll/final pay scenarios can be processed without manual cleanup.
workItems.sourceTrace: Primary: PDF payroll processing/page 13; Secondary: BRD transaction discussion on pulling masterfile basic pay and Zero Basic Pay flag.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a regular payroll transaction is created, when eligible employees are generated, then the system pulls active basic pay setup effective for the payroll period.
Given Zero Basic Pay is not enabled, when payroll is computed, then basic pay is included according to pay period, payment mode, and effective date.

Given Zero Basic Pay is enabled, when payroll is computed, then the system does not pull the configured basic pay for that transaction but still allows other selected earnings/deductions based on transaction settings.
Given Zero Basic Pay is enabled, when the user saves the transaction, then the transaction log/audit records that basic pay was intentionally suppressed.
Given a transaction is posted/draft-locked, when basic pay setup changes, then the transaction does not silently change unless reopen/recalculate rules allow it.
qaFocus: Test an employee with active basic pay is included in regular payroll.
Test Zero Basic Pay on transaction with bonuses and loans enabled.
Test posted/draft locked transaction remains stable after masterfile change.

workItems.title: Compute basic pay adjustment and retro pay for effective-dated changes
workItems.need: basic pay changes to generate correct adjustment/retro amounts based on company rules
workItems.benefit: employees receive correct pay when salary changes are effective before or within a payroll period.
workItems.description: As a Payroll Processor basic pay changes to generate correct adjustment/retro amounts based on company rules employees receive correct pay when salary changes are effective before or within a payroll period.
workItems.sourceTrace: Primary: PDF retro pay adjustment/pages 19-21; Secondary: Annex A basic pay adjustment computational basis.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a basic pay increase is effective in a prior period and company rule is Based on Worked Days, when payroll is processed, then adjustment uses adjusted daily rate multiplied by worked days in the affected period.
Given company rule is Based on Unworked Days, when payroll is processed, then adjustment uses the applicable unworked-days logic from the PDF sample and company rules.

Given multiple effective-dated rate changes exist in one payroll period, when payroll is computed, then the system splits the period by effective dates and calculates each segment separately or flags for review if unsupported.
Given retro adjustment is generated, when payroll register is viewed, then the adjustment appears as Basic Pay Adjustment or configured earning type with proper taxable classification and GL mapping.
Given retro computation cannot be completed due to missing timekeeping/workdays, when payroll is computed, then the employee is flagged in an exception report.
qaFocus: Use PDF sample with old monthly 100,000, new monthly 120,000, factor 261, worked days 5, expected adjustment 4,597.70.
Use PDF sample with unworked days 6, expected adjustment 5,517.24.
Confirm taxability/GL of adjustment follows earning setup.

workItems.title: Deduct scheduled company loan amortization in payroll
workItems.need: payroll to deduct the scheduled company loan amount automatically
workItems.benefit: loan deductions are accurate and do not need manual encoding every payroll.
workItems.description: As a Payroll Processor payroll to deduct the scheduled company loan amount automatically loan deductions are accurate and do not need manual encoding every payroll.
workItems.sourceTrace: Primary: PDF payroll processing/deduction setup pages 10-13; Secondary: Project Plan recurring company loan feature.
acceptanceCriteria: Given an employee has an active company loan assignment for the pay period, when payroll is computed, then the scheduled amortization is deducted according to payment frequency.
Given remaining balance is less than scheduled amortization, when payroll is computed, then the system deducts only the remaining balance unless final pay rules say otherwise.
Given a loan is not yet started, already ended, inactive, on hold, or fully paid, then the system does not deduct it and records the reason in computation details or exception report.
Given payroll is recalculated, then the system reverses/recomputes loan deduction without double-counting accumulated payment.
Given the transaction is posted or draft-locked, then loan deductions do not change unless reopened and recalculated by authorized user.
qaFocus: Test monthly loan in semi-monthly payroll according to final frequency rules.
Test remaining balance lower than amortization.
Test a posted transaction remains stable after assignment is edited.

Given an employee is in final pay, when company loan exists, then system follows final pay rule: deduct outstanding balance or monthly amortization based on setup.

Given hierarchy/threshold rule cannot be satisfied, then the employee is included in an exception report with details.

dependencies: payroll transaction engine; RR 29-2025 seeded threshold table; effective-date versioning; MWE/region table for meal allowance
acceptanceCriteria: Given a de minimis type exists, when I configure its ceiling, then the system requires ceiling amount, effective date/version, status, and remarks.
Given the RR 29-2025 ceiling is created, when saved, then the default effective date is Jan. 6, 2026 unless an authorized compliance admin sets a different approved version.
Given the ceiling basis is days, month, semester, annual, percentage of MWE, or combined annual cap, when I save, then the system validates the correct data type and required fields for that basis.

Given a prior ceiling version exists, when payroll is computed for a date before Jan. 6, 2026, then the system uses the prior active version; when computed on/after the applicable effective date, it uses the RR 29-2025 version.
Given multiple versions exist, when payroll computes, then the system uses the ceiling effective for the payroll period/payment date according to final effective-date rule.
Given ceiling amount is blank or negative, then save is blocked.
Given a ceiling is updated after use in payroll, then historical payroll remains tied to the ceiling version used at computation time.
Given a ceiling is inactive, then it is not used for future payroll but remains reportable historically.
qaFocus: Test ceiling exactly zero or blank is rejected unless explicitly allowed.
Test future-dated ceiling does not affect current payroll.
Test historical payroll report shows correct ceiling version.

workItems.title: Enforce non-taxable De Minimis threshold during payroll computation
workItems.need: payroll to apply de minimis thresholds automatically
workItems.benefit: non-taxable and taxable earnings are calculated accurately.
workItems.description: As a Payroll Processor payroll to apply de minimis thresholds automatically non-taxable and taxable earnings are calculated accurately.
workItems.sourceTrace: Primary: PDF de minimis threshold/page 8; Secondary: BRD confirmation that de minimis threshold is a setting/reference table. Updated: Grant Thornton/BIR RR 29-2025 source effective Jan. 6, 2026.
dependencies: payroll transaction engine; RR 29-2025 seeded threshold table; effective-date versioning; MWE/region table for meal allowance
acceptanceCriteria: Given an employee receives a de minimis earning and has remaining ceiling, when payroll computes, then the earning is treated as non-taxable up to the remaining ceiling.

Given the payroll date is on/after the effective date of RR 29-2025, when payroll computes, then the system applies the updated seeded threshold version and stores that version in computation details.
Given the employee receives both CBA benefits and productivity incentives, when payroll computes, then both amounts consume the same PHP 12,000 annual ceiling.
Given the de minimis type is Daily Meal Allowance, when payroll computes, then the ceiling is calculated as 30% of the employee applicable regional basic minimum wage for the period.
Given the de minimis earning exceeds remaining ceiling, when payroll computes, then the system splits or flags the excess according to final taxable overflow rule.
Given the employee has prior de minimis payments in the same ceiling period, then payroll considers year-to-date/period-to-date totals before applying the current payroll amount.
Given a de minimis earning is tagged as included in daily/hourly rate, then the system applies rate inclusion only as confirmed by final rule.
Given threshold computation fails due to missing setup, then the employee appears in exception report.
qaFocus: Test employee below threshold remains fully non-taxable.
Test excess amount becomes taxable or flagged based on confirmed rule.
Test cumulative annual/period basis includes prior payrolls but excludes reversed/cancelled transactions.

acceptanceCriteria: Given one or more employees reach or exceed a de minimis threshold during computation, then the system displays a modal before final save/post.
The modal must list employee, de minimis type, current amount, prior accumulated amount, ceiling amount, non-taxable portion, excess portion, and action required.
Given no threshold is reached, then the modal is not displayed.
Given the user acknowledges the modal, then the acknowledgement is stored in transaction audit/log with timestamp and user.
Given the user chooses export/download from modal, then the system exports the threshold-reached list for review.
qaFocus: Test multiple employees and multiple de minimis types in one transaction.
Test acknowledged modal does not lose computation detail after refresh.
Test user cannot post payroll without required acknowledgement if final rule requires it.

Integrate all four modules with payroll transaction computation engine
workItems.need: basic pay, company loans, bonuses, and de minimis ceilings to compute in one payroll run

acceptanceCriteria: Given an employee has basic pay, bonus, de minimis earning, and company loan, when payroll is computed, then the transaction produces gross pay, taxable/non-taxable earnings, deductions, net pay, and loan balance updates.

Given payroll is posted/draft locked, then computation values are frozen until authorized reopen/recalculate.
Given reports are generated, then report values match transaction computation details.

Test recalculation does not double count loan accumulated payments.

workItems.title: Build regression test pack and seeded data for Dev/QA
workItems.need: a seeded test pack covering the four modules and edge cases
workItems.benefit: the team can validate every sprint without reinventing test data.
workItems.description: As a QA Lead a seeded test pack covering the four modules and edge cases the team can validate every sprint without reinventing test data.
workItems.sourceTrace: Derived from all primary and secondary sources in this document.
acceptanceCriteria: Given QA starts testing, then seed data includes employees with monthly, daily, hourly, semi-monthly, and no/basic pay scenarios.
Given company loan testing starts, then seed data includes active, future, ended, held, partially paid, fully paid, and overpaid loan scenarios.
Given bonus testing starts, then seed data includes fixed bonus, 13th month formula bonus, performance bonus, taxable bonus, and ceiling-exceeded bonus.
Given de minimis testing starts, then seed data includes below ceiling, at ceiling, above ceiling, missing ceiling, and future-dated ceiling scenarios.
Given regression is run, then expected values are documented and reviewed by BA/Payroll SME.
qaFocus: Regression pack must include positive, negative, boundary, and integration cases.
Every formula edge case must have expected value and source assumption.
Unconfirmed rules should be marked Pending Clarification, not passed silently.
Field list, data type, required/optional behavior, reference table source, and validations are confirmed.
Formula source is identified: PDF, Annex computational basis, project plan, or payroll SME confirmation.
Access roles are known for create, edit, delete/inactivate, upload, view, and export.
Open questions are either answered or accepted as explicit assumptions with owner and due date.
QA has at least one positive, negative, boundary, and integration test case for the story.
Reporting/audit expectations are confirmed for the story.
All acceptance criteria pass in Dev/QA environment.
Unit tests cover formula services, validation services, and edge cases.
QA regression scenarios pass and expected values reconcile to manual calculation sheet.
Audit trail is captured for all write actions and transaction-sensitive computations.
Role-based access controls are validated.
Upload template, validation errors, reports, and exports are working where applicable.
No cross-company/tenant data leakage is observed.
Known unresolved items are documented and approved for carry-forward, not hidden in release notes. Payroll has enough goblins already.
US-DEMIN-001 must validate that seeded benefit types are complete and active/inactive behavior is respected.
US-DEMIN-002 must validate exact seeded threshold amount, ceiling period/basis, effective date, source/version metadata, and old-version audit visibility.
US-DEMIN-003 must validate accumulation by employee and taxable year/month/semester/day as applicable, including combined cap and region-based cap scenarios.
US-DEMIN-004 modal must show the threshold version and basis used, not just a generic threshold exceeded message.
US-DEMIN-005 exports must include threshold version/source so payroll reviewers can trace why a value was treated as non-taxable or taxable/excess.

stories.title: Create and maintain employee basic pay setup
stories.persona: As a P&A Admin or Client Admin
stories.need: to create, view, edit, inactivate, and audit basic pay setup per employee or employee group
stories.benefit: payroll transactions use the correct basic pay rate for each employee.
stories.description: As a P&A Admin or Client Admin to create, view, edit, inactivate, and audit basic pay setup per employee or employee group payroll transactions use the correct basic pay rate for each employee.
stories.sourceTrace: Primary: PDF earning setup/pages 5-9; Secondary: Annex A payroll setup and BRD basic pay discussion.
acceptanceCriteria: Given I have setup access, when I create a basic pay record, then the system requires employee/employee group, payment mode, basic pay amount, effective date, work days/factor days, status, and remarks where applicable.

Given a duplicate active basic pay exists for the same employee and overlapping effective period, when I save, then the system blocks the duplicate or requires end-dating/inactivation of the old setup.
Given the basic pay record is used in a posted payroll transaction, when a user attempts hard delete, then the system prevents hard delete and allows only inactive/end-dated status with audit trail.

Given I update the basic pay amount or effective date, when I save, then the system writes old value/new value audit history and flags related open payroll transactions for recalculation if applicable.
Given I lack edit access, when I open the setup screen, then I can only view/download according to my role rights.
qaFocus: Test valid monthly, daily, hourly, semi-monthly, and weekly setup where reference values are available.
Test overlapping effective dates and confirm system blocks or requires end-dating.
Test inactive records are not pulled into future payroll but remain visible in history/reporting.
Test audit log contains user, timestamp, action, old value, new value, and remarks.

tasks.title: Finalize field list, required/optional rules, reference table sources, and effective-date overlap rules.
Build create/edit/view/inactivate API and UI form for basic pay setup.
Add validation for positive numeric amount, valid payment mode, effective date, and tenant/company isolation.
Implement audit history for all create/update/inactivate actions.
Create functional tests for CRUD, duplicate overlap, required fields, inactive behavior, and role restrictions.
Create regression tests for previously used basic pay records after new effective-dated setup is added.

stories.title: Automatically derive annual, daily, hourly, and per-minute rates
stories.need: the system to compute derived rates from basic pay setup
stories.benefit: earnings, deductions, statutory bases, and reports use one consistent rate source.
stories.description: As a Payroll Admin the system to compute derived rates from basic pay setup earnings, deductions, statutory bases, and reports use one consistent rate source.
stories.sourceTrace: Primary: PDF computation basis/pages 5-8; Secondary: Annex A and BRD pay mode/monthly-to-daily/hourly discussion.
acceptanceCriteria: Given payment mode is Monthly and monthly basic pay is entered, when I save, then Annual Rate = Monthly Basic Pay x 12.
Given Annual Rate and Work Days per Year exist, when I save, then Daily Rate = Annual Rate / Work Days per Year rounded according to payroll decimal rules.
Given Daily Rate and Required Work Hours per Day exist, when I save, then Hourly Rate = Daily Rate / Required Work Hours per Day and Per-Minute Rate = Hourly Rate / 60.
Given required work days or work hours are missing, when the formula requires them, then the system blocks save or marks rate derivation incomplete with clear error messaging.
Given payment mode is not monthly, when the rate is saved, then the system uses the configured computational basis for that payment mode and stores the derived rate values used by payroll.
qaFocus: Use sample: Monthly Basic Pay 20,000; Work Days 261; Required Hours 8; expected Daily Rate roughly 919.54 and Hourly Rate roughly 114.94.
Test leap/yearly factor changes where Work Days per Year changes.
Test a rate update after an open payroll transaction and ensure transaction can be recalculated or flagged.

tasks.title: Confirm rounding precision for annual, daily, hourly, and per-minute rates.
Implement rate computation service callable by setup, transaction, and report modules.
Add payment-mode formula mapping using computational basis/reference table.
Store computed rates with source fields and formula version used.
Validate monthly to annual/daily/hourly/minute examples and rounding.
Validate missing factor days/hours error behavior and edge cases.

stories.title: Pull basic pay into payroll transaction with Zero Basic Pay option
stories.need: payroll transactions to pull configured basic pay by default and allow controlled zero-basic-pay processing
stories.benefit: special payroll/final pay scenarios can be processed without manual cleanup.
stories.description: As a Payroll Processor payroll transactions to pull configured basic pay by default and allow controlled zero-basic-pay processing special payroll/final pay scenarios can be processed without manual cleanup.
stories.sourceTrace: Primary: PDF payroll processing/page 13; Secondary: BRD transaction discussion on pulling masterfile basic pay and Zero Basic Pay flag.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a regular payroll transaction is created, when eligible employees are generated, then the system pulls active basic pay setup effective for the payroll period.
Given Zero Basic Pay is not enabled, when payroll is computed, then basic pay is included according to pay period, payment mode, and effective date.

Given Zero Basic Pay is enabled, when payroll is computed, then the system does not pull the configured basic pay for that transaction but still allows other selected earnings/deductions based on transaction settings.
Given Zero Basic Pay is enabled, when the user saves the transaction, then the transaction log/audit records that basic pay was intentionally suppressed.
Given a transaction is posted/draft-locked, when basic pay setup changes, then the transaction does not silently change unless reopen/recalculate rules allow it.
qaFocus: Test an employee with active basic pay is included in regular payroll.
Test Zero Basic Pay on transaction with bonuses and loans enabled.
Test posted/draft locked transaction remains stable after masterfile change.

tasks.title: Add basic pay pull logic to payroll transaction generation.
Implement Zero Basic Pay transaction flag and exclusion logic.
Add transaction log entry for zero-basic-pay transactions.
Add recalculation hooks for open/reopened transactions.
Test regular payroll pull, special payroll zero basic pay, draft/post lock, and recalculate behavior.
Validate payroll register shows basic pay as zero only when flag is enabled and authorized.

stories.title: Compute basic pay adjustment and retro pay for effective-dated changes
stories.need: basic pay changes to generate correct adjustment/retro amounts based on company rules
stories.benefit: employees receive correct pay when salary changes are effective before or within a payroll period.
stories.description: As a Payroll Processor basic pay changes to generate correct adjustment/retro amounts based on company rules employees receive correct pay when salary changes are effective before or within a payroll period.
stories.sourceTrace: Primary: PDF retro pay adjustment/pages 19-21; Secondary: Annex A basic pay adjustment computational basis.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a basic pay increase is effective in a prior period and company rule is Based on Worked Days, when payroll is processed, then adjustment uses adjusted daily rate multiplied by worked days in the affected period.
Given company rule is Based on Unworked Days, when payroll is processed, then adjustment uses the applicable unworked-days logic from the PDF sample and company rules.

Given multiple effective-dated rate changes exist in one payroll period, when payroll is computed, then the system splits the period by effective dates and calculates each segment separately or flags for review if unsupported.
Given retro adjustment is generated, when payroll register is viewed, then the adjustment appears as Basic Pay Adjustment or configured earning type with proper taxable classification and GL mapping.
Given retro computation cannot be completed due to missing timekeeping/workdays, when payroll is computed, then the employee is flagged in an exception report.
qaFocus: Use PDF sample with old monthly 100,000, new monthly 120,000, factor 261, worked days 5, expected adjustment 4,597.70.
Use PDF sample with unworked days 6, expected adjustment 5,517.24.
Confirm taxability/GL of adjustment follows earning setup.

tasks.title: Confirm company-level retro computation options and exact worked/unworked-days interpretation.
Implement rate-change detection for payroll periods and prior periods.
Implement worked-days and unworked-days computation paths.
Post result to configured Basic Pay Adjustment earning/pay item.
Recreate PDF sample computations and compare expected Basic Pay Adjustment and Total Basic Pay.
Test missing timekeeping, multiple rate changes, and negative/zero adjustment scenarios.

stories.title: Upload, export, audit, and report Basic Pay setup
stories.need: to upload, validate, export, and report basic pay setup records
stories.benefit: bulk maintenance can be controlled and auditable.
stories.description: As a Payroll Admin to upload, validate, export, and report basic pay setup records bulk maintenance can be controlled and auditable.
stories.sourceTrace: Primary: PDF reports/page 23 and setup pages; Secondary: Project Plan upload/report generation requirements.
acceptanceCriteria: Given I download the upload template, then it contains required fields, sample values, reference value guidance, and validation notes.
Given I upload a file with valid rows, when validation passes, then the system previews row count and creates or updates records only after confirmation.
Given I upload invalid rows, then the system rejects those rows with row-level error messages and does not partially update records unless partial import is explicitly selected.
Given I export setup records, then the report includes current and historical values, status, effective dates, computed rates, and audit metadata if authorized.
Given a bulk upload changes records used by open payroll transactions, then impacted transactions are flagged for recalculation/review.
qaFocus: Test upload with duplicate employee/effective date in same file.
Test upload with invalid payment mode/reference value.
Test export before and after inactivation/end-dating.

Validate export values, filters, and audit metadata.

stories.persona: As a P&A Admin or Client Admin
stories.need: to configure company loan types and accounting mappings
stories.benefit: loan deductions can be standardized and reported correctly.
stories.description: As a P&A Admin or Client Admin to configure company loan types and accounting mappings loan deductions can be standardized and reported correctly.

acceptanceCriteria: Given I have setup access, when I create a company loan type, then the system requires loan code, loan name, loan type/category, GL mapping where applicable, payment frequency defaults, status, and remarks.

Given the loan type has been used in payroll, when a user attempts hard delete, then the system blocks deletion and allows inactive/end-date only.
Given the loan type is inactive, then it is not selectable for new assignments but remains visible in historical reports.
Given a code already exists for the same company, when I save, then the system blocks duplicate active codes.

stories.need: to assign company loans to employees or selected employee groups
stories.benefit: payroll can deduct the correct amortization for each employee.
stories.description: As a Payroll Admin to assign company loans to employees or selected employee groups payroll can deduct the correct amortization for each employee.
stories.sourceTrace: Primary: PDF assignment rule/pages 9 and 12; Secondary: Annex salary-related pay item field list.
acceptanceCriteria: Given an active company loan type exists, when I assign it to an employee, then I can input start date, end date, amount/amortization, payment frequency, principal, interest, total loan, reference number, date granted, and description where applicable.
Given an employee group is selected, when employees under the group load, then I can include/exclude specific employees before saving.

Given the assignment has a hold date/status, then payroll deduction is suspended or flagged according to final hold rules.
Given assignment data is incomplete or invalid, then the system blocks save with field-level error messages.

stories.title: Deduct scheduled company loan amortization in payroll
stories.need: payroll to deduct the scheduled company loan amount automatically
stories.benefit: loan deductions are accurate and do not need manual encoding every payroll.
stories.description: As a Payroll Processor payroll to deduct the scheduled company loan amount automatically loan deductions are accurate and do not need manual encoding every payroll.
stories.sourceTrace: Primary: PDF payroll processing/deduction setup pages 10-13; Secondary: Project Plan recurring company loan feature.
acceptanceCriteria: Given an employee has an active company loan assignment for the pay period, when payroll is computed, then the scheduled amortization is deducted according to payment frequency.
Given remaining balance is less than scheduled amortization, when payroll is computed, then the system deducts only the remaining balance unless final pay rules say otherwise.
Given a loan is not yet started, already ended, inactive, on hold, or fully paid, then the system does not deduct it and records the reason in computation details or exception report.
Given payroll is recalculated, then the system reverses/recomputes loan deduction without double-counting accumulated payment.
Given the transaction is posted or draft-locked, then loan deductions do not change unless reopened and recalculated by authorized user.
qaFocus: Test monthly loan in semi-monthly payroll according to final frequency rules.
Test remaining balance lower than amortization.
Test a posted transaction remains stable after assignment is edited.

tasks.title: Implement payroll loan deduction engine.
Add eligibility checks for start/end/hold/status/frequency/balance.
Persist computation details per employee per loan per transaction.
Implement safe recalculation without duplicate accumulated payments.

Validate payroll register and employee deduction details.

stories.title: Maintain loan accumulated payments and balances
stories.need: the system to maintain computed and manual accumulated payments and loan balance
stories.benefit: loan reporting and future deductions are accurate.
stories.description: As a Payroll Admin the system to maintain computed and manual accumulated payments and loan balance loan reporting and future deductions are accurate.
stories.sourceTrace: Primary: PDF deduction setup/end balance pattern; Secondary: Annex salary-related pay item field list and Project Plan loan balance requirement.
acceptanceCriteria: Given payroll posts a loan deduction, when the transaction is finalized, then accumulated computed payment increases by the posted loan deduction amount.
Given a manual accumulated payment is entered by authorized user, then the system records it separately from computed accumulated payment and recalculates balance.
Given accumulated payments equal or exceed total loan, then the loan status becomes fully paid or inactive for future payroll deduction.
Given accumulated payments exceed total loan, then the system blocks save/post or creates an overpayment exception depending on final rule.
Given a payroll transaction is reversed/reopened/cancelled, then computed accumulated payment is reversed or flagged to prevent double-counting.
qaFocus: Test manual payment before payroll run reduces scheduled remaining balance.
Test balance exactly zero marks loan fully paid.
Test audit trail for manual adjustments.

tasks.title: Confirm precedence of manual vs computed accumulated payment and overpayment handling.
Implement balance ledger for loan deductions and manual adjustments.
Implement fully paid status transition and stop-deduction logic.
Add reversal/cancel/reopen impact handling.
Test ledger movements from posted payroll, manual payment, reversal, and overpayment.

stories.title: Apply loan priority, minimum take-home pay, and final pay handling
stories.need: company loans to follow configured hierarchy and special final pay rules
stories.benefit: deductions comply with client policy and avoid unintended negative net pay.
stories.description: As a Payroll Admin company loans to follow configured hierarchy and special final pay rules deductions comply with client policy and avoid unintended negative net pay.
stories.sourceTrace: Primary: PDF deduction/payroll pattern; Secondary: BRD loan hierarchy and minimum take-home pay discussion.
acceptanceCriteria: Given company deduction hierarchy is configured, when payroll is computed, then lower priority loans are deferred or partially deducted first when net pay is insufficient.

Given minimum take-home pay percentage is configured, when scheduled deductions would reduce net pay below the threshold, then the system adjusts lower-priority loan deductions to meet the threshold where possible.
Given an employee is in final pay, when company loan exists, then system follows final pay rule: deduct outstanding balance or monthly amortization based on setup.
Given loan deduction is partially deferred, then the system records deferred amount and carries it forward or flags it according to final business rule.
Given hierarchy/threshold rule cannot be satisfied, then the employee is included in an exception report with details.
qaFocus: Use scenario: Basic Pay 100,000, required net 40,000, computed net 20,000, loans include SSS/HDMF/car loan; validate defer/partial logic.
Test priority order: statutory first, government loans, company loans, then company deductions as configured.
Test final pay full outstanding balance vs monthly amortization modes.

tasks.title: Confirm hierarchy setup source, final pay options, deferred amount behavior, and negative net pay policy.
Integrate loan deduction with hierarchy/minimum take-home computation service.
Implement final pay rule branch for outstanding balance vs amortization.
Add deferred/partial deduction tracking.
Test BRD scenario where net pay must remain 40% of basic pay and lower-priority loans are deferred.
Test final pay with insufficient net pay and multiple loan priorities.

acceptanceCriteria: Given an employee receives a de minimis earning and has remaining ceiling, when payroll computes, then the earning is treated as non-taxable up to the remaining ceiling.

Given the employee receives both CBA benefits and productivity incentives, when payroll computes, then both amounts consume the same PHP 12,000 annual ceiling.
Given the de minimis type is Daily Meal Allowance, when payroll computes, then the ceiling is calculated as 30% of the employee applicable regional basic minimum wage for the period.
Given the de minimis earning exceeds remaining ceiling, when payroll computes, then the system splits or flags the excess according to final taxable overflow rule.
Given the employee has prior de minimis payments in the same ceiling period, then payroll considers year-to-date/period-to-date totals before applying the current payroll amount.
Given a de minimis earning is tagged as included in daily/hourly rate, then the system applies rate inclusion only as confirmed by final rule.
Given threshold computation fails due to missing setup, then the employee appears in exception report.

document.text: Project Atlas Payroll User Stories
Focused scope: Basic Pay, Company Loan, Bonus Setup, and De Minimis Ceiling Setup | Azure DevOps-ready backlog
Prepared for: Dev and QA teams

Purpose: Convert the payroll requirements, PDF screen/design references, BRD walkthrough notes, and project planning sources into a structured backlog that can be entered into Azure DevOps as Epic > Feature > User Story > Task.

rows: Important source handling note The PDF is treated as the first ground source of truth for design and functional patterns. Where the PDF does not contain a dedicated detailed screen for a requested module, this document explicitly fills the gap using the Phase 2 Project Plan, Annex A, and BRD walkthrough transcripts. This is not a bug in the doc. It is a source gap that Dev/QA should track as an open validation item. Update added: The uploaded "Updated De Minimis Benefits Threshold - Grant Thornton" PDF is now treated as the latest de minimis threshold source for the De Minimis Ceiling Setup. It overrides older conflicting assumptions, especially the prior CBA/productivity incentive threshold and generic/unknown period treatment.

document.text: Document Map
Section 1 - Source basis, source hierarchy, scope, and known source gaps.
Section 2 - What Dev/QA must know before building: module rules, calculations, UI/design, integration, reporting, audit, and QA considerations.
Section 3 - Azure DevOps backlog hierarchy: Epic, Features, User Stories, and Tasks.
Section 4 - QA regression matrix and Definition of Done.
Appendix - Primary PDF visual references used as design/source grounding.
Section 7 - De Minimis regulatory update from the new Grant Thornton/BIR RR 29-2025 source.
1. Source Basis and Scope Control
1.1 Source hierarchy used

rows: Payroll - Earning/Deduction Setup and Payroll Computation.pdf
Used as the first basis for the flow, setup screens, field expectations, classifications, computation options, assignment rules, payroll processing, retro pay samples, and reporting expectations.

Used to confirm that Basic Pay setup, Company Loans, Bonus Setup, and De Minimis ceiling setup are in payroll Phase 2 scope and to capture ADO-like feature descriptions, upload/reporting expectations, and actor access.
Used for field-level setup details, computational basis examples, bonus computation mapping, salary-related pay item data points, and reference table behavior.
BRD Deep Dive and Payroll Walkthrough transcripts
Used to add practical computation behavior, transaction behavior, hierarchy of deductions, minimum take-home pay scenarios, bonus formula behavior, and basic pay rate conversion details.
Updated De Minimis Benefits Threshold - Grant Thornton PDF (Tax Notes, 19 Jan 2026; BIR RR 29-2025)
Used to update the De Minimis Ceiling Setup default benefit types, ceiling amounts, ceiling periods/bases, effective date, QA boundary cases, and ADO story acceptance criteria.

document.text: 1.2 Focused modules only

rows: Out of Scope for this backlog

Government statutory table build, SSS/PhilHealth/HDMF table maintenance, payroll calendar full build, gross-up, take-home pay setup as a standalone feature, final pay full build, retirement setup, remittance monitoring, payslip designer.
Government loan setup except where needed for comparison or hierarchy rules.
All earnings beyond bonus unless they are needed as shared earning setup rules.
All non-taxable earning types beyond de minimis except where needed for classification and reporting.

document.text: 1.3 Source traceability and gaps

rows: Annex A computational basis; BRD 2024-11-21 Part 2 basic pay rate/payment mode discussion
PDF covers earning setup and retro/basic pay adjustment concepts, but detailed basic pay masterfile design is mostly from Annex/transcript.
PDF p. 3 flow, pp. 10-12 deduction setup and assignment patterns
Project Plan company loans; Annex salary-related pay item; BRD loan hierarchy/minimum take-home pay discussion
PDF does not contain a dedicated Company Loan detailed setup slide. Company Loan field and balance requirements are primarily from project plan/Annex/transcripts.
PDF pp. 4-9 earning setup and computation basis pattern
Project Plan bonus setup; Annex bonus computation mapping; BRD bonus formula behavior
Bonus is treated as an earning type; its UI should follow Earning Setup with bonus-specific schedule/formula fields.
PDF pp. 5 and 8: non-taxable classification, De Minimis, threshold, modal warning
Project Plan De Minimis ceiling per type/reference table; BRD 2026-03-19 confirmation of de minimis threshold/list; Updated Grant Thornton/BIR RR 29-2025 threshold note effective Jan. 6, 2026.

Threshold values and periods for the listed de minimis benefits are now grounded on the updated source. Remaining gaps are implementation choices: date basis for applying versions, company/client override permission, and taxable overflow handling.

document.text: 1.4 Actors and access model assumed
P&A Admin and Client Admin are the primary actors for setup and maintenance of pay items, company loans, bonus setup, and de minimis ceilings.
Users with view-only permissions should be able to view/download setup records and reports but not add, edit, delete, upload, or import records.
Role-based access should follow existing Atlas access rights patterns. All write actions must create audit/history records.
Employee-facing visibility depends on Display in Employee View setup flags for earnings/deductions where applicable.

document.text: 2.2 Basic Pay setup - detailed considerations
path: 2. What Dev and QA Need to Know Before Building

rows: Maintain employee basic pay setup and ensure payroll transactions can compute derived annual, daily, hourly, and per-minute rates consistently based on payment mode, work days per year/factor days, and required work hours.

Employee/employee group, payment mode/frequency, basic pay amount, effective date, work days per year/factor days, required work hours per day, status, remarks, created/updated by, audit trail, optional upload reference.

At minimum, support the payroll frequencies listed in Annex/source materials: hourly, daily, weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly, monthly, piece rate, consultancy, and annualized/direct where applicable. Final enum must follow reference table values.

For monthly setup: Annual Rate = Monthly Basic Pay x 12; Daily Rate = Annual Rate / Work Days per Year; Hourly Rate = Daily Rate / Required Work Hours per Day; Per Minute Rate = Hourly Rate / 60. Other payment modes must map through computational basis rules.

Payroll transactions should pull basic pay from employee masterfile/payroll data by default. A transaction-level Zero Basic Pay option means payroll should not pull the configured basic pay for that transaction.
If a new basic pay is effective within or before the pay period, the system must use the correct rate for computation and retro adjustments. Historical records must remain auditable.

Retro pay adjustment can be based on worked days or unworked days depending on company rules. Sample PDF computation uses monthly salary increase, factor/work days, and worked/unworked days to compute Basic Pay Adjustment.
Basic pay feeds earnings computation, statutory basis, withholding tax basis, bonus formulas, minimum take-home pay, final pay, retirement, payroll register, and employee 201/payroll master data.
Validate rate derivation, effective-date selection, zero basic pay behavior, payroll transaction pull, recalculate/update behavior, retro calculation, upload validation, and report output.
path: 2. What Dev and QA Need to Know Before Building

document.text: 2.3 Company Loan setup - detailed considerations
path: 2. What Dev and QA Need to Know Before Building

rows: Configure and assign company loans so payroll can deduct scheduled amortization, maintain balances, and apply loan priority/minimum take-home/final pay rules.
Loan code, loan name, loan category/type, GL breakdown/name/sub-GL, taxable/non-taxable if relevant, payment frequency, default amortization rule, active/inactive status, remarks.

Pay item/loan selected, amount/amortization, start date, end date, description, date granted, reference number, principal, interest, total loan, accumulated payment manual, accumulated payment computed, balance, hold date/status.

Total Loan = Principal + Interest unless total amount is directly provided and approved. Scheduled deduction = configured amortization amount/frequency, capped by remaining balance. Balance = Total Loan - accumulated paid amounts.
Company loans can be fixed/one-time or recurring. Recurring loans deduct until balance reaches zero or end date is reached, subject to hold date, priority, and minimum take-home pay rules.

Loans and company deductions may be ordered by per-company hierarchy. Mandatory statutory/tax items normally come first; government loans and company loans are ordered after statutory items based on setup.
If company policy requires a minimum net pay percentage, the system should defer or partially deduct lower-priority loan deductions to keep net pay at or above the configured threshold.

Final pay may require full outstanding balance or monthly amortization depending on setup. If net pay is insufficient, hierarchy and special final pay rules determine partial/differed deduction and possible negative net handling.

Validate loan creation, employee assignment, recurring deduction schedule, remaining balance, zero-balance stop, partial deduction, hierarchy, minimum take-home pay, final pay, upload/import, and reports.
path: 2. What Dev and QA Need to Know Before Building

document.text: 2.4 Bonus setup - detailed considerations
path: 2. What Dev and QA Need to Know Before Building

rows: Configure bonus pay items, formulas, basis, schedule, taxability, and assignment so payroll can compute fixed and scheduled bonuses correctly.

Bonus should follow the Earning Setup pattern because Bonus is an Earning Type. Required earning fields include code, name, earning type, GL mapping, capped setting, adjust-if-absent if applicable, auto-compute, computation basis, taxability, work days if applicable, display in employee view, and assignment.

Bonus type, bonus schedule/frequency, fixed amount or formula, computation basis, eligibility period, effective start/end, included/excluded employee groups, taxable/non-taxable classification, ceiling applicability.
13th Month Pay can use YTD Earned Basic Pay / 12 months. Performance Bonus can use Tenure in Year x Current Basic Pay. Final formulas must come from the Computational Basis module/reference setup.
Bonus can be taxable or non-taxable depending on setup. 13th month pay and other bonuses may be subject to a non-tax exemption ceiling, defaulted to PHP 90,000 in the Project Plan source.
Bonus formula may be preconfigured and used for automatic computation during payroll transactions where bonus is included. Manual override should be controlled by access rights and audit.
Validate formula selection, fixed amount, scheduled eligibility, group/employee assignment, taxability, non-tax ceiling, caps, upload template, reports, and audit trail.
path: 2. What Dev and QA Need to Know Before Building

document.text: 2.5 De Minimis ceiling setup - detailed considerations
path: 2. What Dev and QA Need to Know Before Building
2.5 De Minimis ceiling setup - detailed considerations

rows: Maintain de minimis benefit types and their ceiling amounts so payroll can classify the non-taxable portion and identify amounts exceeding the ceiling.
De Minimis is a non-taxable earning classification under Earning Setup. The De Minimis list/type is expected to be a reference table and can be expanded as needed.

De Minimis type/code, name/description, ceiling amount, ceiling period (annual/monthly/per payroll as confirmed), effective date/version, active/inactive status, include in daily/hourly rate flag, remarks.

Ceiling is the limit where de minimis can be calculated as non-taxable. The primary PDF states that upon reaching the ceiling, the system should prompt a modal informing the user that certain employees reached the configured threshold.

Recommended processing rule for Dev/QA validation: amount up to ceiling remains non-taxable; excess amount should be reclassified to taxable earnings or flagged for payroll review, depending on final client confirmation.
PDF explicitly marks confirmation needed for whether including de minimis in daily/hourly rate also causes the earning to be affected by absences, tardiness, or leaves.
Validate per-type threshold, modal warning, threshold reached list, taxable/non-taxable split or review flag, effective-date versioning, upload/report, and integration with payroll register.

The new Grant Thornton document summarizes BIR RR 29-2025 and states that revised de minimis thresholds are effective January 6, 2026. The system should seed/version these thresholds with that effective date.

The setup must support the 11 categories in RR 29-2025: private unused vacation leave, government vacation/sick leave credits, medical cash allowance to dependents, rice subsidy, uniform/clothing allowance, actual medical assistance, laundry allowance, employee achievement awards, Christmas/anniversary gifts, overtime/night-shift daily meal allowance, and combined CBA/productivity incentive.

Ceiling period is not one-size-fits-all. Some caps are annual/taxable year, some monthly, one semester/month equivalent, one days-based, one region/MWE percentage-based, and one combined annual cap across two benefit types.
Old and new thresholds must coexist. Payroll should use the threshold version effective for the applicable payroll/taxable period, and posted transactions should retain the threshold version used.
The old CBA/productivity incentive assumption of PHP 10,000 should be replaced with PHP 12,000 combined annual value under the updated source.

Monthly cap; validate against payroll period/pay frequency.

Requires employee work region and MWE/minimum wage lookup effective for the payroll period. Formula-driven, not fixed amount.

document.text: 2.6 Cross-module calculation rules and formulas
path: 2. What Dev and QA Need to Know Before Building

rows: Annual Basic Pay = Monthly Basic Pay x 12
Used when employee basic pay is configured monthly.
Daily Rate = Annual Basic Pay / Work Days per Year
Work days/factor days come from company/employee setup.
Hourly Rate = Daily Rate / Required Work Hours per Day
Required hours must be captured or defaulted from work schedule/company rule.
Used for minute-based computations where enabled.
Current Basic Pay x 12 / Work Days in a Year x Actual Number of Days Worked
Based on Annex computational basis example for Basic Pay Adjustment.
Adjusted Daily Rate x worked days in affected period
PDF worked-days sample shows salary increase divided by factor/work days, then multiplied by worked days.
Adjusted Daily Rate x unworked days before effective date
PDF unworked-days sample treats days before effective date as not yet entitled to new rate.
Total Loan = Principal + Interest, unless a direct total is supplied and approved
Store both manual and computed values where the source fields exist.
Balance = Total Loan - Accumulated Payment (Manual + Computed as applicable)
Need clear precedence if manual accumulated payment overrides computed.
Deduction = min(Scheduled Amortization, Remaining Balance), then adjusted by hierarchy/minimum take-home pay if applicable
Tenure in Year x Current Basic Pay
One absolute ceiling, default PHP 90,000 unless configured otherwise
Project Plan source states one ceiling regardless of bonuses requiring ceiling.
Non-taxable amount = min(Current De Minimis Amount, Remaining Ceiling); Excess = amount over ceiling
Overflow treatment must be confirmed: taxable split vs review flag.
Use the ceiling record where Effective Date <= applicable payroll/taxable date and Status = Active; retain the selected version in payroll computation details
Confirm whether the applicable date is payroll period end date, payout date, or taxable date.
Remaining Ceiling = 12,000 - YTD(CBA benefits + productivity incentive benefits)
CBA and productivity incentives must consume one combined annual cap.
Per-day ceiling = Regional Basic Minimum Wage x 30%
Requires MWE/region lookup effective for the period and employee work region.
Private employee monetized unused vacation leave non-taxable limit = 12 days/year
Track by employee/taxable year in days or converted hours according to setup.

document.text: 2.8 Data, integration, and audit expectations
path: 2. What Dev and QA Need to Know Before Building

rows: All setup records must have tenant/company scope, unique business code, status, effective start/end where applicable, created/updated metadata, and audit trail.
Earning Type, Deduction Type, Bonus Type, De Minimis Type, GL Breakdown, GL Name, Sub GL, Payment Frequency, Work Days, Employee Group, Taxability, and Unit should be reference controlled.
Transaction creation should pull eligible setup data as of the payroll period/cutoff. Recalculate behavior must refresh transaction details after setup/masterfile changes if allowed.
Standard reports must include payroll register fields and totals; customized reports should allow selected payroll entry fields only unless expanded later.
Track create/edit/delete/inactivate/upload/import/recalculate/use-in-transaction events with user, role, timestamp, old value, new value, and remarks.
Write actions require P&A Admin or Client Admin privileges. View/download privileges can be separate from edit/upload privileges.
All records must be tenant-safe: no cross-client visibility, no shared mutable configuration unless explicitly marked generic/global.

rows: Payroll Pay Item Setup and Compliance Computation Readiness

Build the setup foundation for Basic Pay, Company Loans, Bonus Setup, and De Minimis Ceiling so payroll transactions can compute earnings, deductions, loans, taxable/non-taxable splits, reports, and audit-ready records consistently.
Payroll admins can configure pay-related rules once, assign them accurately, process payroll with fewer manual adjustments, and give QA a traceable computation basis for regression.

document.text: 3.2 Feature summary

rows: Configure basic pay, derive rates, apply effective dates, support zero basic pay transactions, and feed payroll computation.
Company Loan Setup and Balance Management
Configure company loans, assign to employees/groups, deduct amortization, maintain balances, and support hierarchy/minimum take-home rules.
Configure bonus pay items, formulas, schedules, taxability, non-tax exemption handling, and transaction processing.
De Minimis Ceiling Setup and Threshold Enforcement
Maintain de minimis benefit types/ceilings, enforce non-taxable limits, warn users, and support reporting/audit.
Cross-Module Integration, Reports, and QA Regression
Validate that all four features integrate with payroll transaction, reports, security, upload/import, audit, and regression packs.

document.text: 4. QA Regression Matrix

rows: Input monthly pay, work days, required hours
Derived rates match expected formula and rounding.
Create old and new basic pay; run retro
Basic Pay Adjustment matches worked/unworked day rule.
Basic pay not pulled; audit records flag.
Active loan with total, amortization, frequency
Loan deducts scheduled amount and updates computed accumulated payment.
Deduction capped at 1,000 and loan marked fully paid.
Lower priority loan is deferred/partial to meet threshold.
Final pay transaction with active loan
Deduct outstanding balance or amortization per setup.
Bonus = YTD earned basic pay / 12 unless configured otherwise.
System stops applying amount after cap is reached.
Excess is taxable or flagged per final rule.
De minimis amount below remaining ceiling
Non-taxable amount equals ceiling; warning behavior follows final rule.
Modal shows affected employee and excess portion.
Recalculate updates eligible values without double counting.
Posted values remain frozen unless reopened by authorized user.
Action blocked and no data change occurs.
Audit contains old/new value, user, timestamp, remarks.
Totals reconcile to transaction details and subtotals.
Load default seeded de minimis thresholds effective Jan. 6, 2026
All 11 benefit categories, ceiling values, periods/bases, and effective date match the updated source.
CBA 8,000 + productivity incentive 5,000 in the same taxable year
Only 12,000 remains non-taxable; excess 1,000 is taxable or flagged according to overflow rule.
Daily meal allowance regional MWE cap
Regional basic minimum wage configured; daily meal allowance exceeds 30%
System computes 30% regional MWE ceiling and flags/splits excess.
Only 400 is non-taxable or system flags 100 excess.
Payroll before and after Jan. 6, 2026 with old and new threshold versions
Before date uses old version; after date uses RR 29-2025 version; posted results retain version used.

document.text: 5. Definition of Ready and Done
5.1 Definition of Ready
Field list, data type, required/optional behavior, reference table source, and validations are confirmed.
Formula source is identified: PDF, Annex computational basis, project plan, or payroll SME confirmation.
Access roles are known for create, edit, delete/inactivate, upload, view, and export.
Open questions are either answered or accepted as explicit assumptions with owner and due date.
QA has at least one positive, negative, boundary, and integration test case for the story.
Reporting/audit expectations are confirmed for the story.
5.2 Definition of Done
All acceptance criteria pass in Dev/QA environment.
Unit tests cover formula services, validation services, and edge cases.
QA regression scenarios pass and expected values reconcile to manual calculation sheet.
Audit trail is captured for all write actions and transaction-sensitive computations.
Role-based access controls are validated.
Upload template, validation errors, reports, and exports are working where applicable.
No cross-company/tenant data leakage is observed.
Known unresolved items are documented and approved for carry-forward, not hidden in release notes. Payroll has enough goblins already.
6. Open Questions and Clarifications to Close
path: 6. Open Questions and Clarifications to Close

rows: Should every basic pay setup be employee-only, group-based, or both? If both, which one wins if employee and group setup conflict?
What is the official rounding precision for annual, daily, hourly, and per-minute rates?
When masterfile basic pay changes after transaction creation, should automatic synchronization happen or only manual Recalculate?
Exact behavior for multiple effective-dated basic pay changes within one payroll period.
Should manual accumulated payment add to computed payment, override it, or be stored as adjustment only?
How should overpayment be handled: block, exception, or carry as receivable/refund?
For final pay, which company loan types deduct outstanding balance vs monthly amortization?
If minimum take-home pay prevents deduction, should deferred loan amount automatically carry forward?
Final list of bonus types and exact formulas to expose in Computational Basis.
Should bonus cap be annual, per payroll, per bonus type, or lifetime?
Treatment of excess over 13th month/other bonus non-tax ceiling: auto taxable split or exception/review?

Ceiling periods/bases are now seeded from RR 29-2025. Confirm only: applicable date basis for selecting a threshold version, whether clients may override seeded values, and whether previous threshold versions remain globally visible or client-scoped.
Should include-in-daily/hourly-rate also make de minimis affected by absences, tardiness, or leaves?

If de minimis threshold is reached, is modal warning informational only or must block posting until acknowledged/resolved? Also confirm whether excess is automatically split to taxable earning or requires manual reviewer action.
Standard report exact formatting, groupings, file formats, and passphrase handling for exports.
For the government employee vacation/sick leave credit item, confirm whether Atlas must support government-client payroll or keep this as a reference-only seeded type.
For Daily Meal Allowance, confirm which MWE/region source controls the 30% threshold and how employee region is determined if work location differs from company address.
Confirm whether CBA and productivity incentives should be set up as two earning types mapped to one shared ceiling bucket, or one combined de minimis type.
path: 6. Open Questions and Clarifications to Close

document.text: 7. Addendum - Updated De Minimis Benefits Threshold Source
path: 7. Addendum - Updated De Minimis Benefits Threshold Source

document.text: This addendum updates the De Minimis Ceiling Setup portion of the backlog using the newly provided Grant Thornton Tax Notes PDF. The document states that BIR RR 29-2025 amended the maximum de minimis benefit amounts that are exempt from income tax on compensation and fringe benefit tax, and that the updates took effect on Jan. 6, 2026. This means Dev and QA should treat the values below as seeded/default regulatory thresholds, not generic placeholder values.
path: 7. Addendum - Updated De Minimis Benefits Threshold Source

document.text: 7.1 Implementation impact summary
path: 7. Addendum - Updated De Minimis Benefits Threshold Source

rows: Add/confirm all 11 de minimis benefit categories from RR 29-2025.
Validate type, label, active status, and mapping to Earning Setup > Non-taxable > De Minimis.
Posted payroll must store threshold version used. Older versions should remain for audit/history.
This replaces older CBA-only/10,000 assumptions from earlier notes.
Use 30% of basic minimum wage per region.
Requires MWE/region lookup and test data for at least two regions.
Support days, amount, month, semester/month equivalent, annual, combined annual, and percentage basis.
Do not force all de minimis thresholds into one annual amount field.
Show benefit type, threshold version/source, ceiling basis, accumulated amount, non-taxable amount, excess amount, and payroll transaction reference.
Needed for reviewer traceability and QA reconciliation.
path: 7. Addendum - Updated De Minimis Benefits Threshold Source

document.text: 7.2 Updated threshold table for requirements, development, and QA
path: 7. Addendum - Updated De Minimis Benefits Threshold Source
7.2 Updated threshold table for requirements, development, and QA

rows: Monetized unused vacation leave credits - private employees
Not exceeding twelve (12) days during the year
Annual / taxable year; unit is days
Store unit as Days, not amount only. Validate accumulated monetized VL days per employee per taxable year.
Monetized vacation and sick leave credits - government officials/employees
Monetized value of vacation and sick leave credits paid to government officials and employees
Government employee rule; no numeric ceiling stated in uploaded note
Keep as a separate de minimis/reference type. Add client applicability flag if Atlas payroll is used for government entities.
PHP 2,000.00 per employee per semester or PHP 333.00 per month
Validate by employee and period. UI should capture whether the company uses semester or monthly tracking.
PHP 2,500.00 or one (1) sack of 50 kg rice per month amounting to not more than PHP 2,500.00
Allow amount or in-kind equivalent. If in-kind, still store taxable value up to PHP 2,500/month.
Annual accumulation by employee. Excess should split/flag as taxable based on overflow rule.
Used for medical/healthcare needs, annual medical/executive check-up, maternity assistance, and routine consultations.
Monthly cap; validate against payroll period/pay frequency.
Annual monetary value not exceeding PHP 12,000.00
Annual / taxable year plus award conditions
Must support conditions: length of service/safety achievement, cash/gift certificate/tangible property, established written plan, non-discriminatory.
Not exceeding PHP 6,000.00 per employee per year
Annual cap by employee. May occur once or multiple times but cumulative cap applies.
Daily meal allowance for overtime work and night/graveyard shift
Not exceeding 30% of the basic minimum wage on a per-region basis
Requires employee work region and MWE/minimum wage lookup effective for the payroll period. Formula-driven, not fixed amount.
Combined annual monetary value from both CBA and productivity incentive schemes must not exceed PHP 12,000.00 per employee per taxable year
CBA and productivity incentive must share one combined cap. Update older 10,000 assumption to 12,000 from this source.
